title_001: The Sensitivity Meter — How loud are your senses?
html_004:
Do you hear the world louder, feel it deeper, and dwell on it longer than most?
      In just 20 questions, we read the
html_009: There’s no right or wrong here — we just chart your own sensitivity onto the meter.
html_011: About 2 min · 20 questions · Results stay on your device
js_002: Physical senses (light · sound · touch · taste · smell)
js_004: Emotion · Empathy (how easily feelings color you)
js_006: Depth of processing (how much you mull and examine)
js_008: Overload (how quickly crowds · stimulation drain you)
js_009: Bright lights or strong sunlight bother you more than they do most people.
js_010: Sudden noises or loud sounds make you flinch, and they stay on your mind.
js_011: You react sharply to textures on your skin — clothing tags, fabric, tightness.
js_012: Caffeine, medicine, or certain foods hit your body harder than they do most.
js_013: You notice subtle differences in smell or taste that others miss.
js_014: Moving music, films, or stories shake you deeply.
js_015: The mood of whoever you’re with transfers straight onto you.
js_016: When someone’s hurting, their feelings feel like your own.
js_017: When you’re hungry or tired, your moods swing hard.
js_018: Beautiful scenery or art has filled your chest to overflowing more than once.
js_019: You spot small changes or details before anyone else does.
js_020: You tend to mull one thing over for a long time and think about it deeply.
js_021: You watch yourself closely so you won’t make mistakes.
js_022: When lots of people are watching, you can’t perform at your usual level.
js_023: Before deciding, you weigh every possible option for a long time.
js_024: When tasks pile up all at once, you feel overwhelmed fast.
js_025: In crowded, stimulating places, you tire out unusually fast.
js_026: After a busy day, you absolutely need quiet time alone to recover.
js_027: When you have to handle a lot in a short time, your mind goes blank.
js_028: When strong stimulation keeps up (loud noise · flashing screens), you just want to get out.
js_035: You’ve got a sturdy filter that most stimulation can’t rattle. You hold your focus even in noisy places, and because feelings don’t sweep you away, you stay calm in a crisis.
js_036: Sometimes you might miss the quiet signals of a more sensitive person. When someone looks off, make “ask one more time, even if they seem fine” a habit.
js_038: Sensitive when it counts, easygoing when it doesn’t
js_039: You’re a flexible meter that adjusts its sensitivity to the moment. You notice the fine details yet know when to let things slide, so you stay balanced in both relationships and work.
js_040: This balance is your strength. Just know that on a bad day your volume can spike — so tend to signals like tiredness and hunger first.
js_042: You lean toward HSP (Highly Sensitive Person)
js_043: You pick up the subtle textures of the world better than most. Feeling small changes · emotions · beauty so deeply is a real gift — but it also means stimulation piles up and tires you out.
js_044: Book a “stimulation-off” slot into each day. Dimming the lights · earphones · a short walk will reset your meter.
js_046: Strong HSP — your volume is wide open
js_047: Your sensory dial is turned all the way up — a high-gain sensor. Your empathy, deep insight, and rich emotions are your own special gift. But you overload easily, so you feel best when you build yourself a “shield.”
js_048: Sensitivity isn’t a defect — it’s a spec. On high-stimulation days, set aside recovery time in advance, and tell yourself, “This is simply how I’m built to feel.”
js_049: Take the light · noise · texture around you into your own hands. Sunglasses, noise-canceling, and comfortable fabrics can cut a day’s fatigue way down.
js_050: Draw a “boundary line” between other people’s feelings and your own. Repeating “this is their emotion, and I am safe right now” dials down the over-immersion.
js_051: When thinking drags on, set a timer — “just five more minutes, then I decide” — to cut off the mulling. Done protects your mind better than perfect.
js_052: Between packed plans, book yourself “15 minutes of doing nothing” in advance. Recovery isn’t laziness — it’s maintenance time for a high-gain sensor.
jst_006: channel volume is your loudest — care for it like this

_app.title: The Sensitivity Meter
_app.desc: How loud are your senses? 20 questions read your Sense · Empathy · Thought · Environment in real time.
